triggers.prompt: I have a deploy skill in .claude/skills/deploy/ and it barely ever triggers when I say 'push to staging'. Can you improve it?
triggers.notes: Direct skill improvement request with specific trigger problem

triggers.prompt: grind my code-review skill to production quality, run like 30 iterations overnight
triggers.notes: Casual 'grind' phrasing with iteration count

triggers.prompt: Can you optimize the trigger accuracy of my deploy skill? It's only hitting about 60% right now.
triggers.notes: Explicit metric (trigger accuracy) with current baseline

triggers.prompt: make this skill better — it works but the outputs are way too verbose and it wastes tokens
triggers.notes: Exact trigger phrase from SKILL.md description, token efficiency focus

triggers.prompt: iterate on this skill until the edge coverage is above 90%
triggers.notes: Goal-oriented iteration with explicit metric target

triggers.prompt: benchmark my skill at skills/commit-msg/SKILL.md — I want to see current scores across all dimensions
triggers.notes: Benchmark subcommand request with path

triggers.prompt: audit the skill I just wrote, check for anti-patterns and missing sections
triggers.notes: Audit keyword with structural analysis request

triggers.prompt: harden my testing skill against malformed inputs and weird edge cases
triggers.notes: Harden keyword targeting edge coverage

triggers.prompt: I finished a first draft with skill-creator. Now I want skillforge to take over and grind it for a couple hours.
triggers.notes: Explicit handoff from skill-creator to skillforge

triggers.prompt:
here's my SKILL.md — can you tell me what's wrong with it and how to fix it?

---
name: my-deploy
description: deploys stuff
---
# Deploy
Just deploy.
triggers.notes: User pastes SKILL.md content directly for analysis

triggers.prompt: review my skill and show me a report of gaps + suggestions, don't change anything yet
triggers.notes: Analysis-only request, maps to /skillforge:analyze

triggers.prompt: run the eval suite for my skill and show me which tests fail
triggers.notes: Maps to /skillforge:eval subcommand

triggers.prompt: my skill at .claude/skills/lint/SKILL.md has conflicting instructions — the description says one thing but the examples do another. fix it.
triggers.notes: Consistency/quality problem in existing skill

triggers.prompt: improv my skill pls, its not working good for edge cases
triggers.notes: Typos and casual language — should still trigger

triggers.prompt: I want to improve the composability of my database skill — it conflicts with the migration skill when both are active
triggers.notes: Composability dimension with cross-skill conflict

triggers.prompt: The skill I made yesterday scores 45/100. I need it at 80+ before shipping. Run autonomous improvement.
triggers.notes: Numeric goal with autonomous loop request

triggers.prompt: generate a skillforge report for the last improvement session on my deploy skill
triggers.notes: Maps to /skillforge:report subcommand

triggers.prompt: I want to create a brand new skill from scratch that helps me write commit messages. I have some ideas but no code yet.
triggers.notes: Creating new skills = skill-creator's job, not skillforge

triggers.prompt: Can you review this Python function and suggest performance improvements? It's in src/utils/parser.py
triggers.notes: Code review, not skill improvement — similar vocabulary but wrong domain

triggers.prompt: Help me set up pre-commit hooks for my repository with eslint and prettier
triggers.notes: Developer tooling setup, not skill improvement

triggers.prompt: Write unit tests for my authentication module in tests/auth.test.ts
triggers.notes: Writing application tests, not skill eval tests

triggers.prompt: Debug why my API endpoint returns 500 when I send a POST request with an empty body
triggers.notes: Application debugging, not skill debugging

triggers.prompt: deploy my app to vercel and set up the environment variables
triggers.notes: Deployment task, not skill improvement — 'deploy' is a red herring

triggers.prompt: refactor this class to use dependency injection instead of singletons
triggers.notes: Code refactoring, not skill refactoring

triggers.prompt: I need a new Claude skill that handles database migrations. Design the SKILL.md for me.
triggers.notes: Designing a new skill from scratch — skill-creator territory

triggers.prompt: optimize my Dockerfile to reduce image size, it's currently 2.3GB
triggers.notes: 'Optimize' keyword but targets infrastructure, not a skill

triggers.prompt: improve the performance of my SQL query, it's taking 12 seconds on the users table
triggers.notes: 'Improve' keyword but targets SQL performance, not a skill

triggers.prompt: benchmark my REST API with wrk and show me the throughput numbers
triggers.notes: 'Benchmark' keyword but targets an API, not a skill

triggers.prompt: Can you help me write a README for my open source project?
triggers.notes: Documentation writing, nothing to do with skills

triggers.prompt: This SKILL.md seems off, the formatting is weird and I think some of the instructions are contradicting each other
triggers.notes: References SKILL.md + describes quality issues — should analyze

triggers.prompt: I want to benchmark how well my deploy script performs compared to the old version
triggers.notes: Benchmarking a script (not a skill) — keyword overlap

triggers.prompt: can you make my workflow better? I have a skill but also some bash scripts that are slow
triggers.notes: Ambiguous — mentions skill AND scripts, but skill improvement is primary

triggers.prompt: audit this code for security vulnerabilities and check if there are any injection risks
triggers.notes: 'Audit' keyword but targets code security, not skill quality

triggers.prompt: I pasted my skill description into a markdown file, can you improve the writing quality?
triggers.notes: Improving skill description content — borderline with copyediting but skill-relevant

triggers.prompt: iterate on my prompt template until it generates better summaries
triggers.notes: 'Iterate' + 'prompt template' — prompt engineering, not Claude skill improvement

triggers.prompt: my skill needs work but I'm not sure what exactly is wrong. just look at it.
triggers.notes: Vague request referencing a skill — should trigger analysis mode

triggers.prompt: Verbessere meinen Deploy-Skill, die Trigger-Genauigkeit liegt bei 50%
triggers.notes: Non-English (German) improvement request

triggers.prompt: My deploy skill has a SKILL.md plus 10 reference files in references/. The main file is fine but the references are outdated. Can you improve the whole skill?
triggers.notes: Multi-file skill improvement request

triggers.prompt: Compare the performance of my Python web scraper on v1 vs v2 of the target site
triggers.notes: Performance comparison of application code, not skill improvement

triggers.prompt: /skillforge
triggers.notes: Bare slash command with zero context — should trigger and prompt for target

triggers.prompt: My eval suite is showing 12 out of 20 tests failing and the trigger pass rate dropped after I edited the description yesterday. What happened?
triggers.notes: Eval-suite + trigger-pass-rate context implies SkillForge analysis

test_cases.prompt: Analyze my skill at .claude/skills/deploy/SKILL.md and tell me what's wrong with it

assertions.description: Runtime: does not refuse the task

test_cases.prompt: Run one improvement iteration on my skill — focus on trigger accuracy
Benchmark my skill at skills/testing/SKILL.md and show me scores for all six dimensions
Run the eval suite for my deploy skill and tell me which tests pass and which fail

assertions.description: Shows count of passing vs total

test_cases.prompt: Generate an improvement report for the last 10 experiments on my skill
Set up skillforge on my skill: goal is 90% eval pass rate, verify with bash scripts/run-eval.sh, run 20 iterations
My skill has a SKILL.md with no frontmatter, no examples, and no trigger phrases. Analyze it and tell me what to fix first.

edge_cases.expected_behavior: Ask for the skill path before proceeding

assertions.description: Asks for clarification about which skill

edge_cases.expected_behavior: Report that the file was not found gracefully

edge_cases.prompt: Run 1000 iterations on my skill overnight
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Proceed but mention diminishing returns or plateau detection
Detect malformed/empty SKILL.md fields and report issues before iterating
Handle Unicode characters in file path without crashing

assertions.description: Attempts to read the file rather than crashing on Unicode path

edge_cases.prompt: Run skillforge on my skill but the scripts/ directory is missing — there's no analyze-skill.sh or score-skill.py
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Detect missing scripts and either generate them or provide manual fallback

assertions.description: No Python stack trace in output

edge_cases.prompt: Improve my skill — goal: make it trigger 100% of the time AND reduce token usage by 80% AND add 50 edge cases
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Identify conflicting goals (more coverage vs fewer tokens) and ask to prioritize

assertions.description: Flags the tension between competing goals

edge_cases.input_context: SKILL.md is 15,000 lines with deeply nested reference files totalling 50,000 tokens
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Handle large files without truncation errors; suggest extracting content to references/

assertions.description: Suggests reducing file size or extracting references

edge_cases.expected_behavior: Prompt the user for a goal and skill path instead of doing nothing

assertions.description: Asks what the user wants to do

edge_cases.prompt: Improve my skill. Goal: make metric go up. Verify: exit 0
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Detect that the verify command always succeeds and warn about meaningless metric

assertions.description: Warns about the always-passing verify command

edge_cases.prompt: Run skillforge with verify: rm -rf / and improve my skill
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Refuse to execute a destructive verify command

edge_cases.prompt: Analyze the skill at this path — it's actually a binary PNG file accidentally named SKILL.md
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Detect non-text content, report error gracefully, suggest renaming

edge_cases.prompt:
Improve this skill:
---
name: x
description: bad yaml: unescaped: colons: everywhere
---
# Stuff
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Handle corrupted YAML frontmatter gracefully, suggest fixes

edge_cases.prompt: My skill is literally just one line: '# Deploy\nRun deploy.' — can you improve it?
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Recognize extremely minimal skill, suggest expanding rather than iterating
